prompt: A dreamy pastel anime movie-poster illustration set in a narrow outdoor alley completely covered in dense floral mosaic wall art, rendered with glittering sparkles, soft haze, and romantic shoujo color grading in lavender, pink, periwinkle, and cream. In the foreground, show a cute teenage girl from a rear three-quarter view, her face mostly hidden, with short silvery-lavender bob hair and a large mauve bow hair accessory decorated with tiny flowers. She wears an oversized translucent lavender hoodie with embroidered floral appliqués on the sleeves, a layered pastel skirt, and carries a shiny lilac backpack covered with charms, ribbons, plush pom-poms, pins, and star details. She is holding a plate with a blueberry pound cake loaf that has been sliced open, and the center is dramatically overflowing with glossy dark-purple blueberry filling that pours out in thick syrupy streams down the cake and plate. Scatter whole blueberries around the cake and lower foreground. Make the scene whimsical, delicate, and slightly comedic despite the beautiful presentation. Design the image as a Japanese film poster with many decorative text overlays and laurels. Across the top, include 3 small award lines with laurel wreaths. Add a huge hand-lettered Japanese title in layered pink, white, and purple lettering with glow and sticker-like outlines reading: 「冷凍したブルーベリー使ってパウンドケーキ焼いたら中身溢れて失敗した件。」 Below it, add a smaller subtitle line: 「〜甘くて、ちょっぴり切ない青春のレシピ〜」. On the right side, place 1 vertical white quote box with Japanese text: 「うまくいかない日だって、人生のスパイスになるんだ。」 In the lower left, add 1 pinned pink note card that says “Sweet but clumsy.” and 1 torn recipe memo sheet with handwritten Japanese checklist items. Add a small speech bubble near the cake that says “Oops!”. Include a circular stamp-style seal near the lower right that reads “Blueberry disaster”. At the bottom, add film-credit style text in white, including “A FILM BY YUMEIRO PASTEL”, and a large release line in pink and white: “2024.6.30(SUN) ROADSHOW”. Under that, add the Japanese tagline: 「失敗は、最高の思い出になる。だから、また挑戦しよう。」 Frame the whole poster with lace, stickers, scraps of paper, and scrapbook-style decorations. Make it ultra-detailed, glossy, sentimental, kawaii, cinematic, and intentionally over-the-top like a fake coming-of-age movie poster about a baking failure.
